subject: Anthropology
title: The First Cities
description: A 60-second lesson on The First Cities.

hook.text: Uruk had 40,000 people by 3000 BCE — temples, markets, workshops, and streets. The city was born in Mesopotamia.

buildup.text: The first cities arose in river valleys: Mesopotamia, Egypt, the Indus, and China, where irrigation fed large groups.

discovery.text: Cities concentrated people, creating specialization — priests, potters, scribes, and soldiers living side by side.

twist.text: Cities also created inequality, disease, and waste. Dense living brought problems that villages never faced.

climax.text: The first cities were experiments in living together at scale. Their solutions — and failures — still echo today.

quiz.question: Where did the first cities arise?
options: In river valleys with irrigation potential
